name: Roast Loin of Venison with Cranberries
recipeIngredient: 4 lb boneless loin of venison (at room temperature)
2 tablespoons cold butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium nonreactive saucepan, combine the lemon, orange, ginger, sugar, and bay leaf with 1 cup of cold water. Bring to a boil over high heat, stirring to dissolve the sugar. Reduce the heat to moderate and boil, uncovered, until syrupy, 10 to 15 minutes.
2. Stir in the cranberries, then remove from heat and cool. Transfer the mixture to a glass container, cover, and refrigerate for 1 to 2 days, stirring once or twice during that time.
3. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Rub the venison with the olive oil, 1 teaspoon of the salt, 1 teaspoon of the pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon of the chopped juniper berries, pressing the seasonings into the meat.
4. Set the loin on a rack in a roasting pan and roast, basting frequently with the pan juices, until medium-rare (about 135°F on a meat thermometer), 25 to 30 minutes. Cover the venison loosely with foil and set aside for 10 to 15 minutes before carving.
5. Meanwhile, remove and discard the bay leaf and the lemon, orange, and ginger slices from the cranberries. In a food processor or blender, puree half the cranberries and half the liquid until smooth.
6. In a medium nonreactive saucepan, boil the wine over high heat until reduced to 1/2 cup, about 5 minutes. Add the stock and bring to a boil. Add the cranberry puree, reduce the heat to low, and simmer, uncovered, until slightly thickened, about 10 minutes.
7. Remove from heat. Strain the remaining whole cranberries and add them to the sauce with the remaining 1/4 teaspoon each of salt, pepper, and chopped juniper berries. Swirl in the cold butter.
8. Slice the venison thinly (stir any juices into the sauce) and serve with the sauce, reheated if necessary.
